---
title: "ECMS"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    fig_caption: yes
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{ECMS}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
---
# Overview
```{r model_family_header, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_header(
  family = "ecms",
  pages = c(
    Overview = "index.html",
    Implementation = "ecms-implementation.html",
    Theory = "ecms-theory.html"
  )
)
```

```{r ecms_overview, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_overview(
  family = "ecms",
  summary = paste(
    "The elastic cylinder modal series solution (`ECMS`) is the package's",
    "solid elastic-cylinder family. It combines the phase-shift treatment of an",
    "infinite elastic circular cylinder with the usual finite-length coherence",
    "factor used near broadside."
  ),
  core_idea = paste(
    "Represent the elastic interior by longitudinal and transverse cylindrical",
    "waves, determine the phase shifts induced by the boundary conditions, and",
    "apply the near-broadside finite-length coherence factor to recover",
    "monostatic backscatter."
  ),
  best_for = c(
    "Solid elastic finite cylinders near broadside",
    "Backbone-like cylindrical structures where shear-wave support matters",
    "Canonical elastic-cylinder benchmarks and component models"
  ),
  supports = c(
    "`ESS` or compatible elastic-cylinder scatterers",
    "Longitudinal and transverse elastic wave speeds in the cylinder interior",
    "Straight and current bent broadside branches"
  ),
  assumptions = c(
    "Circular cylinder with homogeneous elastic properties",
    "Broadside or near-broadside incidence",
    "Finite-length treatment through a coherence factor rather than a fully general 3D elastic solve"
  ),
  reading = c(
    "[Implementation](ecms-implementation.html): object setup and current comparison workflow",
    "[Theory](ecms-theory.html): elastic cylindrical potentials, phase shifts, and finite-length closure"
  )
)
```
